title: Tutorial using sudo when already su

**Describe the results you received:**

I see a lot of:

# sudo

In the tutorials. But # assumes you are already root so why do the 'sudo'?

**Describe the results you expected:**
Either 

$ sudo 

or s/sudo //

comments.body: Your a git expert now, open a PR to change the `# to a $`
